parsed_review.summary: Independent read-only review of CP00-707 (RP23 External Integrations II finance-integrations P04 fixture/evidence + P05 contract bridge, risk C, 150 units RP23.P04.M06.S05-RP23.P05.M03.S08). The pack-level production_ready claim (descriptor-only, no-write, no-real-data, runtime-closed) is supported by the evidence. Verified: (1) Baseline coherence — pack_id/risk/unit_count/range/next-boundary are identical across registry binding, requirements, manifest, plan_binding_snapshot, review-baseline, contract current_pack, and plan live_state (CP00-707 latest, hands off to CP00-708 / RP23.P05.M03.S09). (2) Deliverable distribution independently counted from the 150 baseline units = ui:38, claude_review:13, security_audit:14, implementation:39, fixture:30, test:10, hermes_evidence:6, matching the prompt exactly. (3) Implementation (registry/service/validators/index) is descriptor-only with a comprehensive NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION; validators.js actively enforces (not merely attests) descriptor-only rows, no raw/financial/credential payloads, no bank/card/WEHAGO/Douzone/tax/DART runtime opening, no permission-decision/cross-tenant/audit-body leakage, synthetic-only data, and authority-boundary invariants. (4) Tests (model.test.js) cover CP707 binding/coverage, descriptor validation errors==[], runtime-closed Hermes/Claude packets, and contract projection latest=CP00-707; command-evidence reports the full 23-command matrix + npm test + build at exit 0, and all referenced npm scripts exist in package.json. (5) The regenerated 1.8MB contract shows current_pack/latest_pack CP00-707 with zero validation:false entries and no runtime-opened/product-write/real-financial-data flags set true. (6) Authority boundary intact — claude_is_final_approval=false, claude packets read_only=true with promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, human approval required for runtime opening, local validation does not claim enterprise trust, and Hermes is used only for descriptor/evidence-packet shape (emits_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. (7) Review-hardening preconditions satisfied: auth preflight passed, prompt 2677B < 25600B, schema present, only Read/Grep/Glob tools, no forbidden tools, summary-only prohibited and not used. (8) Scope discipline correct — unrelated dirty/untracked files (including docs/ldip-integration/.../c-ldip-01-invalid-attempt-01.json) are excluded from pack scope. (9) No credentials/secrets/real financial data in source. (10) Lifecycle files (claude-review-result/construction-inspection/adjudication) are honestly in pending_review with all gate flags false — no premature production_ready claim. Two P3 informational notes (non-blocking) recorded below. No P0/P1/P2 issues. blocks_goal_closeout=false: this CP keeps runtime closed and defers enterprise trust to human approval, so the expected authority boundary does not block this CP's closeout goal.

findings.title: Contract validator performs a build-time repo write; distinct from the product-state no-write attestation
findings.details: The RP23 contract validator regenerates and writeFile()s contracts/external-integrations-ii-contract.json (~1.8MB) on each run. This is a deterministic control-plane / repo-artifact write and is fully consistent with the pack's NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION, which enumerates product-state surfaces only (creates_database_rows, updates_database_rows, writes_object_storage, writes_audit_event, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt, writes_state_transition, persists_*). It does not write product state, DB, object storage, audit, or Hermes runtime receipts. Verified the regenerated artifact contains no validation:false entries and no runtime-opened/product-write/real-financial-data flags set true.
findings.recommendation: No action required. Optionally keep the doc/attestation wording explicit that the no-write boundary refers to product state, not to build-time regeneration of the descriptor contract, so the claim is not misread as 'the validator performs no filesystem writes at all.'
findings.post_review_disposition: Accepted as informational and non-blocking. The write is deterministic control-plane regeneration of contracts/external-integrations-ii-contract.json; CP00-707 no-write attestation continues to mean no product-state, database, object storage, audit, Hermes runtime, credential, or financial payload writes.

findings.title: Pack lifecycle/adjudication files remain in pending_review placeholder at review time
findings.details: claude-review-result.json (status pending_review, valid_review_receipts:[], exactly_one_valid_pack_level_claude_review_run:false, null finding_counts), construction-inspection.json (verdict PENDING_REVIEW, production_ready/pack_production_ready/pack_level_claude_review_completed/descriptor_verified all false), and adjudication.md (all findings pending) are correctly in pre-review placeholder state because this review is the gating step. This is the expected flow, not a defect, and is self-consistent with the adjudication disposition requiring exactly one valid closeout-eligible hardened review receipt before queue advancement.
findings.recommendation: Before flipping production_ready and advancing the queue to CP00-708 / RP23.P05.M03.S09, ensure closeout records exactly one valid review receipt from this run and propagates the verdict and finding counts into claude-review-result.json, construction-inspection.json, and adjudication.md. Reject any second/duplicate or hardening-invalid receipt (auth failure, 0-byte stdout, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing required fields).
findings.post_review_disposition: Satisfied by this closeout adjudication: the valid review receipt, verdict, finding counts, construction inspection, and adjudication records are propagated before queue advancement. Non-blocking and expected at review time.
